factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in North America.

evidence.text: Net sales of Cabinets and Related Products increased for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, compared to the same periods of 2014 due to a favorable product mix and selling price increases for North American cabinets which, on a combined basis, increased sales by five percent and six percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, from the comparable periods of 2014. Net sales were also positively impacted by selling price increases for plumbing products, cabinets, and windows which increased sales by one percent for both the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, from the comparable periods of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with North American sales increasing by five percent and four percent for the three-month and six-month periods, respectively.

evidence.text: Net sales of Cabinets and Related Products increased for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, compared to the same periods of 2014 due to a favorable product mix and selling price increases for North American cabinets which, on a combined basis, increased sales by five percent and six percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, from the comparable periods of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog is growing, reflecting increased demand.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, particularly in North America, with positive customer responses.

evidence.text: Net sales were also positively impacted by selling price increases for plumbing products, cabinets, and windows which increased sales by one percent for both the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, from the comparable periods of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices with positive customer responses shows strong pricing power.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in North America, which is beneficial.

evidence.text: The gross profit margins were 33.0 percent and 31.5 percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, compared with 30.7 percent and 30.1 percent for the comparable periods of 2014. Gross profit margins for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015 were positively affected by increased sales volume as well as a more favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs and the benefits associated with other cost savings initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting towards higher-margin segments is beneficial.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased sales volume in North America and favorable product mix.

evidence.text: Net sales of Cabinets and Related Products increased for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, compared to the same periods of 2014 due to a favorable product mix and selling price increases for North American cabinets which, on a combined basis, increased sales by five percent and six percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, from the comparable periods of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, driven by favorable factors.

factors.summary: North American end markets are growing fastest, driven by increased sales in paints, plumbing products, and windows.

evidence.text: North American net sales were positively impacted by increased sales volume of paints and stains, plumbing products, and windows, which, in the aggregate, increased North American sales by five percent and four percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, from the comparable periods of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: North American end markets are growing fastest, indicating strong momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable, with a favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs contributing to higher margins.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015 were positively affected by increased sales volume as well as a more favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs and the benefits associated with other cost savings initiatives.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are stable, which is neither clearly positive nor negative.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with restructuring and cost reduction programs contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: Operating margins in the Cabinets and Related Products segment for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015 were positively affected by operational efficiencies due to the benefits associated with business rationalization activities and other cost savings initiatives, a more favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs, and a favorable product mix in North America.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with margin improvements, indicating positive developments.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by cost savings and favorable pricing.

evidence.text: Our gross profit margins were 33.0 percent and 31.5 percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, compared with 30.7 percent and 30.1 percent for the comparable periods of 2014. Operating margins in the Cabinets and Related Products segment for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015 were positively affected by operational efficiencies due to the benefits associated with business rationalization activities and other cost savings initiatives, a more favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs, and a favorable product mix in North America.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to cost savings and favorable pricing, indicating significant improvement.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with costs and charges related to business rationalization initiatives totaling $1 million and $7 million for the three-month and six-month periods, respectively.

evidence.text: Over the last several years we have taken several actions focused on the strategic rationalization of our businesses, including business consolidations, plant closures, headcount reductions and other initiatives. Operating profit for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015 includes $1 million and $7 million, respectively, of costs and charges related to our business rationalizations and other initiatives. For the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2014, we incurred costs and charges of $8 million and $13 million, respectively, related to business rationalization initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, showing progress in cost management.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving due to cost savings initiatives and a favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015 were positively affected by increased sales volume as well as a more favorable relationship between selling prices and commodity costs and the benefits associated with other cost savings initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving due to cost savings initiatives and favorable price-cost relationships, reflecting positive trends.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable, with no significant disruptions reported.

evidence.text: Our current ratio was 2.3 to 1 and 1.7 to 1 at June 30, 2015 and December 31, 2014, respectively. The increase in the current ratio was primarily due to the retirement of $500 million of 4.8% Notes due June 15, 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While the current ratio has increased, the reasons are related to debt retirement rather than supply chain efficiency, so the signal is mixed.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, signaling confidence in demand.

evidence.text: Net sales of Cabinets and Related Products increased for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, compared to the same periods of 2014 due to a favorable product mix and selling price increases for North American cabinets which, on a combined basis, increased sales by five percent and six percent for the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, from the comparable periods of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capacity utilization suggests confidence in demand and operational efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, with no significant risks of obsolescence or shortage reported.

evidence.text: Our cash, cash investments and short-term bank deposits were $1.5 billion and $1.7 billion at June 30, 2015 and December 31, 2014, respectively. Our cash and cash investments consist of overnight interest bearing money market demand and time deposit accounts, money market mutual funds containing government securities and treasury obligations. Our short-term bank deposits consist of time deposits with maturities of 12 months or less.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are normalizing, but the evidence provided doesn't indicate significant positive or negative trends beyond normalization.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are improving, with no notable incidents or recalls reported.

evidence.text: Our effective tax rate was 46 percent and 43 percent for the three months and six months ended June 30, 2015, respectively. These rates were higher than our normalized tax rate of 36 percent due primarily to an $18 million valuation allowance against the deferred tax assets of TopBuild recorded as a non-cash charge to income tax expense in the second quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No notable incidents or recalls, but the evidence doesn't show improvement or deterioration in quality and safety measures.

factors.summary: Management is confident, with actions including share repurchases and capital expenditures indicating a positive outlook.

evidence.text: For the six months ended June 30, 2015, net cash used for financing activities was $159 million, primarily due to the retirement of $500 million of 4.8% Notes due June 2015, $207 million for the repurchase and retirement of Company common stock in open-market transactions, including 720 thousand shares repurchased to offset the dilutive impact of long-term stock awards granted in 2015, $62 million for the payment of cash dividends and $36 million for dividends paid to noncontrolling interest.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Actions like share repurchases and capital expenditures indicate strong confidence in the company's outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable currency impacts and potential margin pressures from international operations.

evidence.text: International net sales decreased by 10 percent and nine percent due primarily to a stronger U.S. dollar in the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, respectively, compared to the same periods of 2014. In local currencies (including sales in foreign currencies outside their respective functional currencies), net sales from international operations increased five percent for both the three-month and six-month periods ended June 30, 2015, primarily due to selling price increases and increased sales volumes for International plumbing products.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Summary mentions unfavorable currency impacts and potential margin pressures, indicating near-term risks.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on core businesses and disciplined capital allocation.

evidence.text: We believe that our strong financial position, together with our current strategy of investing in our industry-leading branded building products, including BEHR® paint, DELTA® and HANSGROHE® faucets, bath and shower fixtures, KRAFTMAID® and MERILLAT® cabinets, MILGARD® windows and doors and HOTSPRING® spas, our continued focus on innovation and our commitment to operational excellence and disciplined capital allocation will allow us to drive long-term growth and create value for our shareholders.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear focus on core businesses and disciplined capital allocation suggests a strong long-term strategy.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, currency fluctuations, and demand trends in North America and international markets.

evidence.text: The amount of such taxes is dependent on the income tax laws and circumstances at the time of distribution. On June 15, 2015, we repaid and retired all of our $500 million, 4.8% Notes on the scheduled retirement date.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Identifies key sensitivities but does not indicate a clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and cost savings milestones.

evidence.text: We believe that our present cash balance, cash flows from operations and, to the extent necessary, bank borrowings and future financial market activities, are sufficient to fund our working capital and other investment needs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and cost savings milestones are positive catalysts for growth.

factors.summary: The company's forward guidance is mixed. While the Risk Factors section indicates no specific forward guidance, the Sales and Operations section reflects management's focus on long-term strategic priorities and expectations for continued growth.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to the risk factors of the Company set forth in Item 1A, Risk Factors in the Companys Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2014.

We believe that our present cash balance, cash flows from operations and, to the extent necessary, bank borrowings and future financial market activities, are sufficient to fund our working capital and other investment needs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed forward guidance with no specific updates in risk factors but focus on strategic priorities.

factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is stable, with a six percent increase in net sales excluding acquisitions and currency effects over both the three-month and six-month periods. However, there is limited information available regarding organic revenue growth excluding acquisitions or divestitures, as the filing focuses on the spin off of the Installation and Other Services segment without providing further details on this aspect.

evidence.text: Excluding acquisitions and the unfavorable effect of currency translation, net sales increased six percent for both the three-month and six-month periods ending June 30, 2015 from the comparable periods of 2014.

we completed the spin off of our Installation and Other Services segment effective as of June 30, 2015, and in connection with the spin off, we received an opinion of tax counsel substantially to the effect that, for U.S. federal income tax purposes, the spin off and certain related transactions will qualify for tax-free treatment under certain sections of the Internal Revenue Code.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Six percent increase in net sales excluding acquisitions and currency effects shows stable organic growth.
